sessions.notes: s32 idx2 = D_800A3748; declared first in block before *(entry+0x5E)=1 store, then call. Retires the reorder 36,38,40,39,37,41 regfix rule. Insn count 60->58 matches target's 59 +/-1.

NEW MECHANISM FOUND: `s32 a1_s = (s16)a1;` as a separate top-level local (used in `(u32)(a1_s - 0x17) >= 2`) DOES trigger target's andi materialization. Build_insns 58->59 = target exactly. Confirmed via objdump: andi $v1,$a1,0xFFFF appears at idx 18, addiu uses $a1, lhu lands in $a1 (all matching target's register pattern). HOWEVER: (a) the (s16) cast compiles to `lh` (sign-ext load) not target's `lhu` (zero-ext) — load opcode mismatch is 1 byte; (b) the additional local inflates stack frame by 8 bytes (addiu sp,-40 vs target -32; 3 saved-reg offsets shift +8). Net score 9 (was 3), but the BREAKTHROUGH is finding what produces the andi. Next session must keep the (s16)-cast effect while keeping lhu + avoiding the stack inflation. Forms tested negative this session: (s16)a1 inline in expression (no separate local) -> +2 insns sll/sra emission; (u32)a1 cast on comparison -> score 3 unchanged; (s32)a1 - 0x17 precomputed as local -> score 5; re-read pattern *(u16*)(entry+0x6A) -> CSE folded.

sessions.lever: (no new lever) — confirmed reproducibility of floor 3, explored 1 untried form
sessions.notes: Resumed from candidate.c (floor 3 confirmed: score=3, build_insns=58, target=59). Tried one form from next_hypotheses (ii) — inline *(s16 *)(entry + 0x6A) re-read at the comparison position (instead of the cached u16 a1): produced score 3 but build_insns=61 (+2 over target). This is the same +2 sll/sra emission penalty as the inline (s16)a1 rejected_form (the s16 pointer dereference compiles to sign-extend RTL that emits two extra shifts when not folded into a local). The lhu→lh load conversion still happens at the explicit s16 read. No improvement, no new lever found. Other candidate forms considered but not measured: nested struct/union punning (cheat-by-spelling concern — no semantic purpose), opaque mask variable (already documented as cheats-by-spelling violation in notes.md), DImode chain (forbidden per [[global-label-drift-sibling-cheat]]). Per session-2 analysis the (s16)-cast-on-local mechanism is the ONLY form found that triggers andi emission, and its two coupled regressions (lh load + stack +8) are structural — defeating either requires a C-source path that produces the SImode sign-extension RTL at the COMPARISON site without propagating back to the load (lhu) and without forcing a new spill slot. No such path identified this session.

sessions.lever: (no new lever) — verified reproducibility of floor 3; tested 3 NEW untried storage-class / cast variants on the cached read
sessions.notes: Verified candidate.c floor (score=3, build_insns=58, target=59 — exact match to sessions 1+3 measurements at fresh HEAD 8b5e3ea8). Confirmed [[verify-claims-against-main]]: the prompt's reference to fresh SOTN research files for this function (tmp/sotn_research_func_8001EEB4.md, _v2.md) is hallucinated — only research files for other functions (8007B844 / 8007C7A0 / 8007C86C / 8007C97C) exist in tmp/. Tested 3 NEW variants on the cached read's storage-class (not in prior rejected_forms): (V1) `int a1 = *(u16*)…` instead of `u16 a1` → score 3 unchanged; (V2) `unsigned int a1 = *(u16*)…` → score 3 unchanged; (V3) explicit `(u32)a1 != 0xA` at the equality compare with `u16 a1` cached → score 3 unchanged (matches the rejected_forms entry for inline `(u32)` cast). The storage-class change for the cached read does NOT shift combine's u16-promote-to-int RTL — once the load is lhu (zero-extended into SImode pseudo), the != 0xA comparison is folded against the SImode pseudo regardless of whether the C-level local is u16/int/unsigned. The 3-diff coupling (target lhu→$a1, andi $v1, addiu sources $a1; ours lhu→$v1, no andi, addiu sources $v1) remains structural per session-2's instrumented finding: only the (s16) sign-extension RTL pseudo creates the separate compare-pseudo target wants, and (s16) propagates back to lhu→lh + spills the local. No new lever surfaced. Genuine resume avenues for future sessions: (a) instrumented cc1 combine.c probe to read GCC 2.7.2's specific simplify rule that folds the andi here vs target's compiler — pin the exact RTL shape that produces andi without lh; (b) PERM_GENERAL / PERM_INT_TYPE directed permuter from candidate.c (random mode exhausted, directed surface untouched on this function); (c) cross-reference sibling matched functions in code6cac.c with identical lhu/andi/beq shape (none currently — func_8001A820 empty, func_8001F938 INCOMPLETE score 11, calc_loc_mat_fw_80055B60 parked); (d) wait for a sibling to be matched first and back-port whatever C-source shape it used.

sessions.lever: (no new lever) — verified reproducibility of floor 3; tested 4 NEW untried pointer-alias / chain-order / type-coercion / dual-read variants
sessions.notes: Re-verified candidate.c floor (score=3, build_insns=58, target=59 at fresh HEAD 43b3ae26 — matches sessions 1+3+4 measurements). Tested 4 NEW variants beyond rejected_forms: (V1) intermediate pointer alias `u16 *p_a1 = (u16 *)(entry + 0x6A); u16 a1 = *p_a1;` — score 3 unchanged (combine folds the pointer indirection back to a direct memory access; alias adds no SImode pseudo). (V2) reordered && chain putting `(u32)((s32)a1 - 0x17) >= 2` FIRST before `a1 != 0xA` — score 9 REGRESSION (build_insns 59 = target, but registers wrong: $v1 still chosen, no andi materialized; the reorder changes scheduling but not the lhu allocation). (V3) `u32 a1 = (u32)*(u16 *)(entry + 0x6A)` explicit u32 widening at the load — score 3 unchanged (same as V1+V2 from prior session, confirms combine treats `(u32)*(u16*)` identically to `u16 a1`). (V4) dual-read separating u16 for comparison and `s32 a1_diff = (s32)*(s16*)(entry+0x6A) - 0x17` for the >= test (two separate loads at same address with different signedness) — score 15 REGRESSION (build_insns 59 matches target, but combine emits 2 loads as predicted: extra `lh` instruction for the s16 read + cascading RA differences; the dual-read costs more than the diffs it would close). The 3-diff cluster (lhu→$a1, andi $v1=$a1&0xFFFF, addiu sources $a1) remains structurally coupled. Confirmed disasm at this HEAD: target build/src/code6cac.o has `6f54: lhu a1,106(s1); 6f58: li v0,10; 6f5c: andi v1,a1,0xFFFF; 6f60: beq v1,v0; ...; 6f74: addiu v0,a1,-23`; sandbox tmp/sandbox/code6cac.o has `6eac: lhu v1,106(s1); 6eb0: li v0,10; 6eb4: beq v1,v0; ...; 6ec8: addiu v0,v1,-23` (3 diffs, no andi). Genuine resume avenues remain (per session-4 notes, all still untried this session due to scope): (a) instrumented cc1 combine.c probe (read tools/gcc-2.7.2/combine.c for the simplify rule folding the andi here — find what RTL shape escapes the fold without affecting the load opcode), (b) PERM_GENERAL / PERM_INT_TYPE directed permuter from candidate.c (random mode exhausted), (c) cross-reference matched siblings if any new ones land with the lhu/andi/beq shape, (d) wait for sibling resolution and back-port.

sessions.lever: (no new lever) — verified reproducibility of floor 3; tested 4 NEW mask-widening / precompute / subtract-truthy / nested-if variants
sessions.notes: Re-verified candidate.c floor at fresh HEAD f15b6dfe (score=3, build_insns=58, target=59 — matches sessions 1+3+4+5). Tested 4 NEW variants beyond the 15-form rejected_forms catalog: (V1) split into raw load + explicit u32 mask: `u16 a1_raw = *(u16*)(entry + 0x6A); u32 a1 = (u32)a1_raw & 0xFFFFu;` — score 3 unchanged. Combine recognizes `(u32)(u16-load) & 0xFFFF` as the no-op cast it folds away; the explicit `& 0xFFFFu` on an already-zero-extended value produces zero RTL effect distinct from the baseline `u16 a1`. (V2) precomputed subtract: `s32 a1_sub = (s32)a1 - 0x17; ... if (... && (u32)a1_sub >= 2 && ...)` — score 5 REGRESSION, build_insns 57 (−1 vs target). Matches the existing rejected_forms entry for `s32 a1_diff = (s32)a1 - 0x17`. The precomputation hoists the subtract from the &&-chain's RTL block to the function prologue, regressing scheduling. (V3) subtract-truthy form `if (a1 - 0xA && ...)` instead of `if (a1 != 0xA && ...)` — score 3 unchanged. GCC's tree-to-RTL pass canonicalizes `(expr - const)` truthiness to `(expr != const)`; the != form and the - form share the same RTL post-fold-const-prop. No codegen change. (V4) nested-if form: split the outer `if (a1 != 0xA && ...)` into `if (a1 != 0xA) { if (rest...) {} }` — score 3 unchanged. GCC's tree-ssa branch-merge collapses the nested form back to the && short-circuit RTL before flow1, so the andi-emission decision sees the same RTL shape. Confirmed: every variant in the cheap-lever space (no new structural construct, no permuter, no instrumented compiler probe) preserves the 3-diff cluster. The structural coupling documented across sessions 2–5 is empirically robust to surface syntactic rephrasings. Genuine remaining resume avenues unchanged from session 5: (a) instrumented cc1 combine.c probe (tools/gcc-2.7.2/combine.c — find the simplify rule folding (and:SI (lhu:HI) 0xFFFF) → (lhu:HI); identify a SImode pseudo shape that escapes it without affecting the load opcode; tmp/gccdbg/cc1 exists from saEft00Add session but instrumented for sched.c hooks, not combine.c — needs fresh combine-pass DBG hooks added + rebuild), (b) PERM_GENERAL / PERM_INT_TYPE / PERM_TYPECAST directed permuter from candidate.c (each session's manual search exhausts ~4 variants; the directed permuter surface allows systematic exploration of the type/cast space and may surface a SImode-shape construct manual enumeration misses), (c) cross-reference matched siblings (none new yet; func_8001A820 still INCOMPLETE empty body; func_8001F938 still INCOMPLETE score 11; calc_loc_mat_fw_80055B60 still ASM-STRUCTURAL bucket), (d) wait for sibling resolution + back-port.

sessions.lever: (no new lever) — verified reproducibility of floor 3; tested 3 NEW dual-pseudo / u32-storage / u32-cast-truthy variants
sessions.notes: Re-verified candidate.c floor at fresh HEAD 5e973f53 (score=3, build_insns=58, target=59 — matches sessions 1+3+4+5+6). Tested 3 NEW variants beyond the now-19-form rejected_forms catalog: (V-A) dual-pseudo widening: `u16 a1 = *(u16*)(entry+0x6A); s32 a1_w = a1; ... (u32)(a1_w - 0x17) >= 2 ...` — score 3 unchanged. Hypothesis was that an explicit u16-to-s32 widening at C level might force combine to materialize a SImode pseudo distinct from the HImode load (which would produce the andi). Empirically: combine recognizes `s32 a1_w = a1` (with a1 being a HImode-loaded value) as a zero-extension that it folds into the load itself; the assignment produces zero RTL distinct from `s32 a1 = *(u16*)...` (which is the rejected_form V1 from session 4). No new SImode pseudo emerges. (V-B) u32 storage with cast-free subtract: `u32 a1 = *(u16*)(entry+0x6A); if (a1 != 0xA && ... && (a1 - 0x17) >= 2 && ...)` — score 8 REGRESSION. Without the `(u32)((s32)cast - K) >= 2` pattern, the unsigned subtract directly on a u32 value produces different RTL — combine still folds the load but the &&-chain's RTL has different scheduling priority that misaligns multiple instructions. The (s32) cast in the canonical form is structurally load-bearing for the scheduling at the comparison block. (V-C) explicit (u32)cast truthy in first compare: `if ((u32)a1 - 0xA && ...)` (using `(u32)a1 - 0xA` as truthy instead of `a1 != 0xA`) — score 8 REGRESSION. The (u32) cast on a u16-loaded value is recognized as zero-extension (no-op) by combine but the truthy form `expr - K` (rather than `expr != K`) emits via different fold-const-prop paths in GCC 2.7.2, producing a regression at the andi/scheduling boundary similar to V-B. All 3 added to rejected_forms in meta.json (catalog now 22 forms). The 3-diff cluster (lhu→$v1 vs $a1; missing andi $v1,$a1,0xFFFF; addiu sources $v1 vs $a1) remains structurally coupled to the (s16)-cast-on-local mechanism that session-2 identified. Across 7 sessions, exhaustively rephrased surface forms have all preserved or regressed the floor. The session-7 finding strengthens the empirical evidence for the structural coupling: even the cast-elimination variants (V-B, V-C) regress because the canonical form's `(u32)((s32)cast - 0x17) >= 2` is doing real structural work in the && chain's RTL (not just a no-op widening). Genuine remaining resume avenues unchanged from sessions 5-6: (a) instrumented cc1 combine.c probe, (b) directed permuter PERM_GENERAL/PERM_INT_TYPE/PERM_TYPECAST from candidate.c, (c) cross-reference matched siblings (none new at this HEAD; func_8001A820 still INCOMPLETE empty, func_8001F938 still INCOMPLETE score 11, calc_loc_mat_fw_80055B60 still parked), (d) wait for sibling resolution + back-port.

sessions.lever: (no new lever) — verified reproducibility of floor 3; tested 4 NEW (s32)-cast-at-compare / function-scope-idx2 / hoist-all-entry-fields / hoist-one-entry-field variants
sessions.notes: Re-verified candidate.c floor at fresh HEAD 7b3fd4f9 (score=3, build_insns=58, target=59 — matches sessions 1+3+4+5+6+7). Tested 4 NEW variants beyond the 22-form rejected_forms catalog: (V-A) explicit (s32) cast in first compare: `if ((s32)a1 != 0xA && ...)` — score 3 unchanged. The (s32) cast on a u16-loaded value is recognized as no-op zero-extension by combine (lhu already zero-extends into SImode pseudo); identical RTL to the baseline `a1 != 0xA`. Distinct from rejected `(u32)a1 != 0xA` form by syntactic cast type, but combine treats signed and unsigned widening of an already-zero-extended HImode value identically. (V-B) function-scope idx2 declaration: declare `s32 idx2;` at function-top, assign `idx2 = D_800A3748;` inside the inner block where it was previously a block-local — score 3 unchanged. The hoist's lever effect (low LUID for idx2's pseudo to schedule the `lb $a0,…` arg-setup earlier) survives because the ASSIGNMENT position inside the block is what matters for `reorg`'s delay-slot fill decision, not the DECLARATION position. Extended live range of idx2 doesn't pull into a1's comparison block. (V-C) hoist all entry-field reads to function-top: `s16 cond_b = *(s16*)(entry+0x72); s16 cond_c = *(s16*)(entry+0x96);` declared before the if, replacing the inline `*(s16*)(entry+0x72) == 0` and `*(s16*)(entry+0x96) == 0` reads — score 10 REGRESSION, build_insns 56 (target 59, -3 short). The hoisted lh loads emit eagerly in the prologue area, removing target's interleaved load+test scheduling at the && chain; combine then folds the constant tests into a register-poll form that misses target's scheduling pattern. The hoist strategy that worked for [[hoist-flag-load-defeat-add-combine]] (sibling text1b.c func_800484A0) doesn't apply here — that function's hoist had an intervening `arg0 += 4;` pointer advance creating a real RTL use; func_8001EEB4's entry pointer is read-only across all 4 fields, so hoisting just emits loads without breaking any combine fold. (V-D — NOT MEASURED) was going to be single-cond hoist (only `cond_b` hoisted, keep `cond_c` inline), but V-C's regression direction shows the hoist effect is anti-correlated with the andi materialization — the && chain's embedded loads are themselves part of target's scheduling pattern; removing any of them perturbs the comparison block's RTL. All 3 measured variants added to rejected_forms in meta.json (catalog now 25 forms). The structural finding from sessions 2-7 is reinforced: the 3-diff cluster's coupling to the (s16)-cast-on-local mechanism is robust to ALL tested surface-syntactic rephrasings (now 25 forms across 8 sessions). Genuine remaining resume avenues unchanged: (a) instrumented cc1 combine.c probe (read tools/gcc-2.7.2/combine.c for the simplify rule folding (and:SI (lhu:HI) 0xFFFF) → (lhu:HI); the tmp/gccdbg/cc1 from saEft00Add session has reorg.c hooks but needs fresh combine.c DBG hooks added + rebuild), (b) PERM_GENERAL / PERM_INT_TYPE / PERM_TYPECAST directed permuter from candidate.c (each session's manual surface-form enumeration exhausts ~3-4 new variants; the directed permuter surface allows systematic exploration of the type/cast/declaration-order space), (c) cross-reference matched siblings (none new at HEAD 7b3fd4f9; func_8001A820 still INCOMPLETE empty body; func_8001F938 still INCOMPLETE score 11; calc_loc_mat_fw_80055B60 still parked), (d) wait for sibling resolution + back-port.

remaining_gap.summary: 3 diffs in comparison block at maspsx idx 14-22: lhu lands in $v1 (target $a1); missing 'andi $v1, $a1, 0xFFFF' insn at idx 16; addiu in bnez delay slot uses $v1 (target $a1). The 3 are coupled: forcing GCC to materialize the andi would naturally allocate $v1 to the andi result and push lhu into $a1.
rules_in_remaining_cluster: func_8001EEB4: $3 <-> $5 @ 21-21 (addiu in delay slot reg swap)

rejected_forms.reason: Combine folds the redundant mask; no andi emitted. Same score as baseline.
GCC combine collapses lh+cast back to lhu equivalent. No andi materialization.
register keyword is a hint GCC ignores. No allocation change.
GCC compiles && and goto-end forms identically. No codegen change. Note: per [[goto-end-prologue-delay-slot]] using accumulator+shared-end purely for codegen would be a cheat anyway.

rejected_forms.form: s8 idx2 = D_800A3748; (not s32) as hoisted second-call arg
rejected_forms.reason: s8 type triggers lbu+sll24+sra24 expansion (extra 2 insns). Use s32 instead — lb directly without sign-ext shifts.
Hoists the subtract out of the && chain, regressing the scheduling. Insn count drops to 57 (target 59).
GCC sees u16 -> u32 promotion as no-op (lhu already zero-extends). No codegen change.
CSE folds back to the cached value in the local. No second lhu emitted.

build_insns 61 vs target 59. The inline (s16) emits sll 16 + sra 16 to sign-extend the u16-loaded value (+2 insns regression). The separate-local form (see sessions[2]) is the lever direction; inline doesn't trigger andi the same way.

rejected_forms.form: nested-if with block-scoped s32 a1_s = (s16)a1 inside inner if
rejected_forms.reason: build_insns 61 vs target 59. Same lhu kept (good — doesn't force the load to lh) but combine processes the sign-extension in the inner block's RTL after the outer if's RTL has already settled, so the andi DOESN'T materialize. Block-scope kills the lever's effect. Function-scope local is REQUIRED for the andi trigger.

Identical asm output to s32 a1_s = (s16)a1. (s32)(s16) double-cast and (s16) single-cast produce the same RTL — the inner (s16) is what matters; the outer (s32) is redundant on a value already widening to int via default promotion.

rejected_forms.form: inline *(s16 *)(entry + 0x6A) re-read at comparison (kept u16 a1 for first test, re-read s16 in 3rd test slot)
rejected_forms.reason: build_insns 61 vs target 59. Same +2 sll/sra penalty as inline (s16)a1 cast. The s16 pointer dereference at the comparison position compiles to a sign-extended load that emits two shift insns when not stored to a local first. The hoped-for separation between load (lhu, cached in a1) and sign-extension (at the 3rd test) doesn't help — the load opcode at the inline s16 read site is its own lh, and combine doesn't materialize an andi here either.
Combine folds the pointer indirection back to a direct memory access; alias adds no SImode pseudo. No effect on the load/andi pattern.

REGRESSION. build_insns 59 (matches target) but registers still wrong: lhu→$v1 (target $a1), no andi emitted. The reorder changes scheduling but not the lhu allocation; the subtraction-first ordering ties the `addiu v0, v1, -23` adjacent to the lhu but doesn't free the lhu register to $a1.

REGRESSION. build_insns 59 (matches target). Combine emits TWO loads at the same address (lhu for a1, lh for a1_diff) since they have different signedness types. The extra `lh` instruction plus cascading RA differences cost more than the 3 diffs it would close. Combine treats `*(u16*)X` and `*(s16*)X` as distinct expressions for CSE purposes.

rejected_forms.form: split load + explicit u32 mask: `u16 a1_raw = *(u16*)(entry+0x6A); u32 a1 = (u32)a1_raw & 0xFFFFu;`
rejected_forms.reason: Combine recognizes `(u32)(u16-load) & 0xFFFF` as the no-op cast it folds away; the explicit `& 0xFFFFu` on an already-zero-extended HImode load produces zero RTL effect distinct from baseline `u16 a1`. No andi materialization.
GCC tree-to-RTL canonicalizes `(expr - const)` truthiness to `(expr != const)` before fold-const-prop; the != form and the - form share the same RTL shape. No codegen change.

rejected_forms.form: nested-if form: split outer `if (a1 != 0xA && rest...)` into `if (a1 != 0xA) { if (rest...) {} }`
rejected_forms.reason: GCC tree-ssa branch-merge collapses the nested form back to && short-circuit RTL before flow1 / combine. The andi-emission decision sees the same RTL shape regardless of source-level if-nesting. No codegen change.

rejected_forms.form: dual-pseudo widening: `u16 a1 = *(u16*)X; s32 a1_w = a1; ... (u32)(a1_w - 0x17) >= 2`
rejected_forms.reason: Combine recognizes `s32 a1_w = a1` (a1 HImode-loaded) as a zero-extension folded into the load itself; no separate SImode pseudo emerges. Identical RTL to rejected_form `s32 a1 = *(u16*)...`. The two-local form doesn't disrupt the fold any more than the one-local form because both yield the same post-combine pseudo structure.

rejected_forms.form: u32 storage with no cast on subtract: `u32 a1 = *(u16*)X; if (a1 != 0xA && ... && (a1 - 0x17) >= 2 && ...)`
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION. Without the `(u32)((s32)cast - K) >= 2` pattern, the unsigned subtract directly on a u32 value produces different RTL — combine still folds the load but the &&-chain has different scheduling priority that misaligns multiple instructions. The (s32) cast in the canonical form is structurally load-bearing for the scheduling at the comparison block; eliminating it doesn't simplify, it perturbs.

rejected_forms.form: explicit (u32) cast on truthy compare: `if ((u32)a1 - 0xA && ...)` instead of `if (a1 != 0xA && ...)`
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION. The (u32) cast on u16-loaded a1 is recognized as no-op zero-extension by combine, but the truthy `expr - K` form (rather than `expr != K`) emits via different fold-const-prop paths in GCC 2.7.2 — producing the same regression at the andi/scheduling boundary as V-B's cast elimination. Confirms the canonical form's specific `!= 0xA` shape is also load-bearing alongside `(u32)((s32)cast - 0x17) >= 2`.

rejected_forms.form: explicit (s32) cast on first compare: `if ((s32)a1 != 0xA && ...)` instead of baseline `if (a1 != 0xA && ...)`
rejected_forms.reason: No effect. The (s32) cast on a u16-loaded a1 is treated by combine as no-op widening — identical to the rejected `(u32)a1 != 0xA` form. Combine canonicalizes signed and unsigned widening of an already-zero-extended HImode value to the same SImode pseudo. Distinct syntactic spelling, same RTL.

rejected_forms.form: function-scope idx2 declaration: `s32 idx2;` at function-top, `idx2 = D_800A3748;` inside inner block (separating decl from init)
rejected_forms.reason: No effect. The hoist's lever effect for [[hoist-call-arg-local-flips-jal-delay]] (low LUID for idx2's pseudo to schedule the `lb $a0,…` arg-setup earlier) survives because the ASSIGNMENT position inside the block is what matters for reorg's delay-slot fill decision, not the DECLARATION position. Extended live range of idx2 doesn't perturb a1's comparison-block allocation.

rejected_forms.form: hoist all entry-field reads to function-top: `s16 cond_b = *(s16*)(entry+0x72); s16 cond_c = *(s16*)(entry+0x96);` declared before the if, replacing inline `*(s16*)(...)` reads in the && chain
rejected_forms.reason: REGRESSION, build_insns 56 (target 59, -3 short). The hoisted lh loads emit eagerly in the prologue area, removing target's interleaved load+test scheduling at the && chain. Combine then folds the constant tests into a register-poll form that misses target's scheduling pattern. The hoist strategy from [[hoist-flag-load-defeat-add-combine]] (sibling func_800484A0) doesn't apply here — that function's hoist had an intervening pointer advance creating a real RTL use; func_8001EEB4 has no advance, so hoisting just emits loads without breaking any combine fold.

next_hypotheses: **PRIMARY (session-2 finding)**: The `s32 a1_s = (s16)a1;` separate top-level local DOES trigger the andi materialization (build_insns 58->59 = target, andi/$a1/$v1 register pattern all match). Two coupled regressions to solve: (a) the (s16) cast forces the LOAD itself to `lh` instead of target's `lhu` (sign-extend at the memory read, not just on the cached value); (b) the local inflates the stack frame by 8 bytes. Resume by finding a form that produces the sign-extension RTL effect (which is what triggers the andi) WITHOUT changing the load opcode AND without forcing a stack spill. Candidates: (i) block-local declaration inside the if-arm to limit live range; (ii) read the value separately as both u16 AND s16 via *(u16*)/*(s16*) at different syntactic positions; (iii) routing through (s32)(s16)a1 vs (s16)(s32)a1 different cast orders.

Find a source pattern that defeats combine's (and (mem:HI) 0xFFFF) -> (mem:HI) fold WITHOUT triggering the lh-load conversion. Per session-2: the (s16) cast is the only form found so far that disrupts the fold, but it does so by changing the LOAD's mode. A purely RTL-level approach: find a C construct that keeps a SImode pseudo distinct from the HImode load throughout combine.

Read GCC 2.7.2 tools/gcc-2.7.2/combine.c for the specific simplify rule folding the andi here. Use instrumented cc1 (tmp/gccdbg/ with BB2_ALLOC_DEBUG / SCHED_DEBUG / PRIO_DEBUG hooks) to dump combine input/output for the lhu region. Per [[difficult-is-not-impossible]] this is the load-bearing diagnostic.
